title: dispatch_payment has 13 arguments; #[allow] suppresses legitimate warning
description: dispatch_payment in cli/src/commands.rs takes 13 arguments and carries #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]. The allow is a code smell: the function has grown to hold the entire payment-dispatch context (wallet fvks, MLS state, session map, watch registry, send closure) because those contexts have no dedicated struct. A PaymentDispatchCtx struct holding the read-only references (wallet_fvks, nicknames, local_names, mls, send_fn, watch_registry) would reduce the argument count to a manageable set and make future additions to the payment subsystem cheaper. chat() has 8 arguments with the same suppress; it is less urgent because it is a top-level command function, not an internal helper.
close_reason: dispatch_payment is out of scope for the proxy feature epic.
